text: Yes - the misses reveal a distinct CONCEPTUAL bridge class that shares proof structure, not premises (e.g. Brouwer<->Lawvere via a shared diagonal argument; Lie algebras<->Lie groups via the tangent-space correspondence). An edit-distance probe finds cross-domain proofs only modestly similar (closest ED 0.586), so structure-based detection is hard. The operational-vs-conceptual gap is the project's central open problem.
